SerialPort.description: Use SerialPort class and declare variables to create a COM port device. The variable name will be the device name.
SerialPort.constructor: SerialPort <variableName> = string, int, string, int, float, int, bool, bool, bool

Socket.description: Use Socket class and declare variables to create a TCP/IP communication device. The variable name will be the device name.

MDesicion.description: Use the MDecision class with variable declaration to allow users to configure M-Decision nodes for conditional judgments or to display pop-up messages.

Reset.documentation: Reset all MDecision parameters to the default.

TimeoutDefaultCase.documentation: The default index after the timeout

Case.documentation: Add, delete, or modify the conditional judgement
parameters: int - Condition or the index of the button

TPoint.description: Use TPoint Class and declare variables to create the point names and the point values. The variable name will be the point name.
constructor: TPoint <variableName> = float, float, float, float, float, float
TPoint <variableName> = string, float[], float[], string, string

TBase.description: Use TBase Class and declare variables to create the point names and the point values. The variable name will be the point name.
constructor: TBase <variableName> = float, float, float, float, float, float

parameters: float - Base Value: X(mm), Y(mm), Z(mm), RX(°), RY(°), RZ(°)

ConShift.documentation: Convert a new base value with the base value (the original base teach value) and the shift value, where the shift depends on the position and direction of the original coordinate.
parameters: float[] - Shift Value: X(mm), Y(mm), Z(mm), RX(°), RY(°), RZ(°)

TTCP.description: Use TTCP Class and declare variables to create the point names and the point values. The variable name will be the point name.

ModbusTCP.description: Modbus TCP device class. Variable name becomes device name.

IODDPreset.documentation: Read IODD file and configure preset parameters.

ModbusRTU.description: Modbus RTU device class. Variable name becomes device name.
constructor: ModbusRTU <variableName> = string, int, string, int, float
ModbusRTU <variableName> = string, int, string, int, float, int
ModbusRTU <variableName> = string, int, string, int, float, int, bool, bool, bool

IODDPreset.documentation: Read IODD file and configure preset parameters.

Compliance.description: Compliance class for robot compliance control settings and stop conditions. Provides single axis, teach, multiple axes, and impedance compliance control modes.

Reset.documentation: Reset all the compliance control motion parameters to the default.

Frame.documentation: Set the reference base of the compliance control motion. 1=Tool, 2=Current base.

HighResistance.documentation: Set the resistance on the non-motion direction (X,Y,Z). false=Default, true=High resistance.

Single.documentation: Set the single axis parameters of compliance control motion.
parameters: int - Distance (mm for XYZ, degree for RXRYRZ)

Teach.documentation: Set the teach parameters of compliance control motion.

Multiple.documentation: Set the multiple axes parameters of compliance control motion.

Impedance.documentation: Set the impedance parameters of compliance control motion.

Timeout.documentation: Set the stop condition of timeout. <0=Disable, >=0=Timeout duration in ms. Call with no params to cancel.

DInput.documentation: Set the stop condition of the digital input. Call with no params to cancel.

AInput.documentation: Set the stop condition of the analog input. Call with no params to cancel.

Condition.documentation: Set the stop condition of the conditional expression. Call with no params to cancel.

Start.documentation: Start the compliance control motion. Returns result: 0=Not Working, 1=Working, 2=Timeout, 3=Distance reached, 4=IO triggered, 5=Resisted, 6=Error, 14=Over Speed, 201-206=Various stop conditions.

Stop.documentation: Stop the compliance control motion. Returns result value after compliance control motion stops.

TouchStop.description: TouchStop class for robot touch stop control settings and stop conditions. Supports Compliance, Line, and Force operation modes.

Reset.documentation: Reset all the touchstop control motion parameters to the default except the operation mode and sensor device.

Frame.documentation: Set the reference base of the touchstop control motion. 1=Tool, 2=Current base.

HighResistance.documentation: Set the resistance on the non-motion direction (X,Y,Z). Valid when operation mode is Compliance.

BrakeDistance.documentation: Set the braking distance in mm. Valid when operation mode is Line.

RecordPosPoint.documentation: Set the record touchstop coordinate value to the point name. Call with no params to cancel.
parameters: bool - Record trigger point (false=stop point, true=trigger point)

Single.documentation: Set the single axis parameters of touchstop control motion.

Teach.documentation: Set the teach parameters of touchstop control motion.

AdvSet.documentation: Set the advanced parameters of touchstop control motion. Valid when operation mode is Compliance.

Timeout.documentation: Set the stop condition of timeout. <0=Disable, >=0=Timeout duration in ms. Call with no params to cancel.

DInput.documentation: Set the stop condition of the digital input. Can also set stroke ratio for Compliance mode. Call with no params to cancel.

AInput.documentation: Set the stop condition of the analog input. Call with no params to cancel.

Condition.documentation: Set the stop condition of the conditional expression. Call with no params to cancel.

Resisted.documentation: Set the stop condition of external resistance detection. Valid when operation mode is Compliance. Call with no params to cancel.

FTReached.documentation: Set the stop condition of monitoring force/torque values. Valid when operation mode is Force. Call with no params to cancel.

Start.documentation: Start the touchstop control motion. Returns result: 0=Not Working, 1=Working, 2=Timeout, 3=Distance reached, 4=IO triggered, 5=Resisted, 6=Error, 14=Over Speed, 201-206=Various stop conditions.
parameters: bool - Zero out force sensor before execution (for Force mode)

Stop.documentation: Stop the touchstop control motion. Returns result value after touchstop control motion stops.

GetStoppedPos.documentation: Get the stopped position values after the robot touch-stopped. Returns float[] of coordinates or joint angles.
parameters: int - Coordinate type (0=flange by current base, 1=joint angle, 2=TCP by current base, 3=TCP by RobotBase, 4=end TCP by RobotBase)

GetTriggeredPos.documentation: Get the triggered position values after the robot touch-stopped. Returns float[] of coordinates or joint angles.
parameters: int - Coordinate type (0=flange by current base, 1=joint angle, 2=TCP by current base, 3=TCP by RobotBase, 4=end TCP by RobotBase)

GetMovingDistance.documentation: Get the moving distance of the touchstop motion in mm.

FTSensor.description: FTSensor class for Force Torque Sensor device. Supports TMFT300, OnRobot_HEX-E/H, ROBOTIQ_FT300, SCHUNK_Axia80, SRI_M4313N3C, WACOH sensors.
constructor: FTSensor <variableName> = string, string, float[], float[], float

X.description: The strength value of the X axis

Y.description: The strength value of the Y axis

Z.description: The strength value of the Z axis

TX.description: The torque value of the X axis

TY.description: The torque value of the Y axis

TZ.description: The torque value of the Z axis

Value.description: The XYZ force strength value and torque value array {X, Y, Z, TX, TY, TZ}, Size = 6

ForceValue.description: The XYZ force strength value array {X, Y, Z}, Size = 3

TorqueValue.description: The XYZ torque value array {TX, TY, TZ}, Size = 3

RefCoordX.description: The X-axis strength value measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordY.description: The Y-axis strength value measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordZ.description: The Z-axis strength value measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordTX.description: The X-axis torque value measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordTY.description: The Y-axis torque value measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordTZ.description: The Z-axis torque value measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordF3D.description: The XYZ strength measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordT3D.description: The XYZ torque measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordForceValue.description: The XYZ strength value matrix measured based on the reference base {RefCoordX, RefCoordY, RefCoordZ}, Size = 3

RefCoordTorqueValue.description: The XYZ torque value matrix measured based on the reference base {RefCoordTX, RefCoordTY, RefCoordTZ}, Size = 3

Model.description: The Model name of the F/T sensor

Zero.description: Turn on or off F/T sensor offset. 0=Zero OFF, 1=Zero ON

Open.documentation: Open the connection to the Force Torque Sensor. Returns true on success, false on failure.

Close.documentation: Close the connection from the Force Torque Sensor. Returns true on success, false on failure.

Force.description: Force class for robot force control settings and motions. Used with FTSensor for force/torque-based control.

Reset.documentation: Reset all the force control motion parameters to the default except the sensor name.

Frame.documentation: Set the reference base of the force control motion. 0=Robot base, 1=Tool (default), 2=Current base, 3=Trajectory.

Distance.documentation: Set the moving distance of the force control motion in mm. Available only in SetPoint F/T operation mode. <0=No limit, >=0=Limited distance.

ProtectionSpeed.documentation: Set the speed protection of the force control motion against axial force or torque.

FTSet.documentation: Set the force and torque of the force control motion. Positive/negative values denote force control direction. Can adjust PID control parameters.

Trajectory.documentation: Set the trajectory path of the force control motion. Trajectory F/T operation mode. Use subflow name or customized function.
parameters: string/? - Subflow name or trajectory path function

Timeout.documentation: Set the stop condition of timeout in milliseconds. Available only in SetPoint F/T operation mode. <0=Disable, >=0=Timeout duration. Call with no params to cancel.

AllowPosTol.documentation: Set the stop condition of tolerant position error in mm. <0=Disable, >=0=Distance of tolerant error. Call with no params to cancel.

DInput.documentation: Set the stop condition of the digital input. Call with no params to cancel.

AInput.documentation: Set the stop condition of the analog input. Call with no params to cancel.

FTReached.documentation: Set the stop condition of monitoring force/torque values. Call with no params to cancel.

Condition.documentation: Set the stop condition of the conditional expression. Call with no params to cancel.

Start.documentation: Start the force control motion. Returns result: 0=Not Working, 1=Working, 2=Timeout, 3=Distance reached, 4=IO triggered, 5=Resisted, 6=Error, 14=Over Speed, 201-206=Various stop conditions.
parameters: bool - Zero out force sensor before execution (default true)
bool - Enable tool gravity compensation (default false)

Stop.documentation: Stop the force control motion. Returns result value after force control motion stops.

Point.description: Access point data in the project. Syntax: Point["name"].attribute

Value.description: The coordinate of the point {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

Joint.description: The joint angle {J1, J2, J3, J4, J5, J6}, Size = 6

Pose.description: The pose of the robot {Config1, Config2, Config3}, Size = 3

Flange.description: The coordinate of the flange's center {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

TeachValue.description: The original coordinate of the teaching point {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

TeachJoint.description: The joint angle at the teach point {J1, J2, J3, J4, J5, J6}, Size = 6

TeachPose.description: The original pose of the robot on the teaching point {Config1, Config2, Config3}, Size = 3

Base.description: Access base coordinate data. Syntax: Base["name"].attribute or Base["name", int].attribute

Value.description: The base value {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

Type.description: The type of the base: 'R'=Robot Base, 'V'=Vision Base, 'C'=Custom Base

TeachValue.description: The original teach base value {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

TCP.description: Access TCP (Tool Center Point) data. Syntax: TCP["name"].attribute

Value.description: The value of the TCP {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

Mass.description: The value of mass in kg

MOI.description: The value of the Principal Moments of Inertia {Ixx, Iyy, Izz}, Size = 3

MCF.description: The value of Mass center frame with principle axes w.r.t tool frame {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

TeachValue.description: The original value of the TCP {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

TeachMass.description: The original value of mass in kg

TeachMOI.description: The original value of the Principal Moments of Inertia {Ixx, Iyy, Izz}, Size = 3

TeachMCF.description: The original value of Mass center frame {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

VPoint.description: Access vision point data. Syntax: VPoint["jobName"].attribute

Value.description: The initial coordinate of VPoint {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

BaseName.description: The name of the VPoint base

TeachValue.description: The original job initial coordinate of VPoint {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

IO.description: Access digital and analog I/O. Syntax: IO["moduleName"].attribute. Modules: ControlBox, EndModule, ExtModule0..N, Safety

Robot.description: Access robot status and configuration. Syntax: Robot[0].attribute (index fixed at 0)

CoordRobot.description: TCP coordinate relative to RobotBase {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

CoordBase.description: TCP coordinate relative to current base {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

Joint.description: Current robot joint angles {J1, J2, J3, J4, J5, J6}, Size = 6

BaseName.description: The name of the current base

TCPName.description: The name of the current TCP

Payload.description: The current compensatory payload in kg

CameraLight.description: The lighting of the robot's camera. 0=Off, 1=On

InstantCameraLight.description: The lighting of the robot's camera (Instant Command). 0=Off, 1=On

TCPForce3D.description: Current TCP force as composite of x, y, z in N

TCPSpeed3D.description: Current TCP speed as composite of x, y, z in mm/s

FT.description: Access force/torque sensor data. Syntax: FT["sensorName"].attribute

X.description: The force value of the X axis

Y.description: The force value of the Y axis

Z.description: The force value of the Z axis

TX.description: The torque value of the X axis

TY.description: The torque value of the Y axis

TZ.description: The torque value of the Z axis

Value.description: The XYZ force and torque array {X, Y, Z, TX, TY, TZ}, Size = 6

ForceValue.description: The XYZ force value array {X, Y, Z}, Size = 3

TorqueValue.description: The XYZ torque value array {TX, TY, TZ}, Size = 3

RefCoordX.description: X-axis force based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordY.description: Y-axis force based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordZ.description: Z-axis force based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordTX.description: X-axis torque based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordTY.description: Y-axis torque based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordTZ.description: Z-axis torque based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordF3D.description: XYZ resultant force based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordT3D.description: XYZ resultant torque based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordForceValue.description: XYZ force array based on reference base {X, Y, Z}, Size = 3

RefCoordTorqueValue.description: XYZ torque array based on reference base {TX, TY, TZ}, Size = 3

Model.description: The model name of the F/T sensor

Zero.description: Turn on or off F/T sensor offset. 0=Zero OFF, 1=Zero ON

Env.description: Access current application environment status. Syntax: Env.attribute (no index required)

AppType.description: Application Type: 1=Real Robot, 2=Virtual Robot, 3=TM AI+ AOI Edge
